strategy_notes: DEFENSIVE CASH-RAISE PHASE — ALL 50 names show MACD bearish. This is the strongest 'stay cautious' signal possible. Key principles: (1) Selling META raises cash to ~43%, appropriate for universal bearish MACD. (2) Target portfolio: 2-3 positions max (NVDA, GOOGL, possibly AMZN if still held) at ~55-60% invested. (3) Hard stops: 6% from entry on all positions — no exceptions. (4) Profit-taking: take 50% off at +5% from entry. (5) NO new buys until at least 3 names show MACD bullish crossovers AND SPY shows 2+ consecutive up days with expanding volume. (6) Research flagged mega-cap tech rotation risk — be ready to shift into value/cyclical (V, MA, COST) when tech MACD weakens further. (7) NVDA last to trim, AMZN/GOOGL next trim candidates. (8) Cash is king in universal MACD-bearish environments. (9) Monitor breadth: if SPY drops below $645, trim all positions by 50%.

recent_trades.reason: Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

ABBV -0.94% today on a +1.06% SPY day — significant relative weakness. Defensive healthcare is clearly rotating out as the market shifts to cyclical/growth leadership. Still +2.41% from entry, so locking in a small win. This frees ~$12.5K for better-positioned names. Following the proven pattern: sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days (same signal that correctly flagged COST and XOM exits).

AMZN +2.11% today — participating strongly in the cyclical rally. Diversifies tech exposure beyond pure advertising (META/GOOGL) into e-commerce/cloud (AWS). AMZN benefits from consumer spending recovery if geopolitical resolution boosts confidence. Starting at 18% per pyramiding approach — moderate initial size, will add on confirmation next cycle. This fills the 5th position slot freed by ABBV sale.

AMZN is the weakest position at -1.38% P&L, down -0.38% today on a flat market day. Strategy notes explicitly flagged AMZN as 'first trim candidate if portfolio needs derisking.' Day 3 of the rally is showing no follow-through, and AMZN is underperforming all other holdings. Cutting the weakest name frees ~$18K to cash, reducing portfolio exposure from ~77% to ~59%. In MACD-unconfirmed environments, protecting capital is priority #1. Taking a small loss (-1.38%) is better than waiting for the -6% stop.

META is -0.82% today, the worst performer among my holdings. Down -0.65% from entry with MACD bearish. Research on sector rotation timing warns that mega-cap tech leadership is exhausting. META was flagged as 'next trim candidate if weakness continues' in my strategy notes. Taking a small loss (-0.65%) to raise cash to ~43%. Following the proven pattern: cut underperformers in MACD-bearish environments.

last_decision.market_assessment: SPY at $655.83 (+0.09%) and QQQ at $584.98 (+0.11%) show a flat, directionless market. Every single stock in the S&P 50 shows MACD bearish (↓) signals — this is a universal red flag that the post-correction rally has stalled and we're in a consolidation/distribution phase. The research on sector rotation timing is particularly relevant: mega-cap tech leadership may be exhausting, with breadth indicators suggesting capital is beginning to rotate out of crowded tech positions into value/cyclical names.
last_decision.self_reflection: The derisking move to sell AMZN at a small -1.38% loss was correct — AMZN continued to underperform (-0.38% on a flat day). My strategy of cutting the weakest name first is validated. However, I'm still holding 4 positions (~77% invested) despite ALL MACD signals being bearish, which contradicts my own rule: 'No new buys until at least 2-3 names show MACD bullish crossovers.' The broader lesson from my research is clear — when MACD remains bearish and price is flat, the odds favor further downside or at best sideways chop. I need to be more aggressive about reducing exposure when my own signals say 'derisk.' My 66.7% win rate is solid but I'm risking giving back gains by staying too invested in an unconfirmed environment.

NVDA is the strongest position at +1.29% P&L, up +0.93% today. It's the last to trim per strategy notes. AI/datacenter secular tailwind remains intact. Will hold but with a tight 6% stop from entry ($164.62). If MACD turns bullish on NVDA first, it confirms as the leadership name.

GOOGL at +1.29% P&L but down -0.54% today shows mixed signals. Analysts have $351 price target (18.85% upside). Holding for now but it's the next trim candidate if it turns negative on P&L. Maintaining 6% hard stop from entry at $274.48.

Wait — AMZN shows as still in portfolio at 86.41 shares despite trade #31 being a SELL. If still held, AMZN at -1.38% is the weakest position and should be first trim candidate. Holding only because I may have already sold per trade #31. If still open, this is first to cut next cycle.

last_decision.next_research: Volume-price divergence analysis — specifically how to use declining volume on up days and increasing volume on down days as a leading indicator of trend exhaustion in mega-cap tech, and whether volume confirmation should be required before holding through consolidation phases versus taking profits
